segments.text:
Kitchen
Quiet
Steam on the copper kettle
The kettle whistles like a bird
Sitting at the table
Watching your eyes
Watching your eyes
Searching for the child I used to know
I see the shift in your posture
Maybe it's phase
Maybe it's fear
Maybe it's just the wind in the hall
This house is big enough to heal you
This house is big enough to heal you
Under this roof we make it right
Safe in the walls of the family light
This house is big enough to heal you
Under this roof
Under this roof
Hold tight
Gentle
Bridges built of birch and bone
We don't need a doctor
We don't need a stranger
Just my hand
Only my hand
Touching your shoulder until you change
The way you speak
The way you walk
The way you think when you are alone
I’ll fix the cracks
I’ll fix the glass
I’ll fix the spirit until it fits
This house is big enough to heal you
This house is big enough to heal you
Under this roof we make it right
Safe in the walls of the family light
This house is big enough to heal you
Under this roof
Under this roof
No white rooms
No cold bars
No paperwork or metal cars
Just the living room rug
Just a motherly hug
Just a fatherly word
Until it’s all you’ve heard
Until it’s all you’ve heard
This house is big enough to heal you
This house is big enough to heal you
Under this roof we make it right
Safe in the walls of the family light
This house is big enough to heal you
Under this roof
Under this roof
Safe at home
Safe at home
Under this roof
